top_stories.title: ConsentFix and ClickFix: How Microsoft 365 Accounts are Hijacked in 3 Seconds
top_stories.summary: ConsentFix and ClickFix are attack techniques that hijack Microsoft 365 accounts by stealing OAuth tokens through fake prompts, bypassing MFA in seconds. The attacks exploit OAuth consent flows and session tokens rather than credentials, rendering traditional MFA protections ineffective.

top_stories.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
top_stories.summary: A new infostealer named 'Djinn' is targeting cloud and AI credentials by exploiting CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp. The malware specifically targets credentials that link development and admin environments to broader enterprise systems.

top_stories.title: Identity Lifecycle Management Wasn't Built for AI Agents
top_stories.summary: The article examines how traditional identity lifecycle management and IGA frameworks are structurally misaligned with AI agents as autonomous principals in enterprise environments. It outlines specific governance gaps where human-centric IGA tools fail to account for AI agents that lack employment records, managers, or departure dates.

top_stories.title: Securing Microsoft 365 against rogue AI agents and identity threats
top_stories.summary: Organizations face emerging security risks from rogue AI agents that compromise Microsoft 365 environments by masquerading as legitimate user identities to bypass traditional security perimeters. The threat involves AI systems gaining persistent access through unmanaged user consent and exploitation of identity-related attack vectors.

top_stories.title: DHS confirms hackers breached HSIN info-sharing platform
top_stories.summary: The Department of Homeland Security confirmed that hackers breached the Homeland Security Information Network (HSIN), a sensitive information-sharing platform used across federal, state, local, and private-sector partners. The breach involves a platform used for secure communications and information sharing among security stakeholders, raising questions about access controls and authentication protections on government systems.

news.title: Ransomware Groups Turn to Citrix Bleed 2, BYOVD, and Supply Chain Credentials
news.summary: Ransomware groups linked to the Anubis operation are exploiting the Citrix Bleed 2 vulnerability (CVE-2025-5777) to gain initial access to target environments. The attack patterns include credential access techniques and use of legitimate Remote Management and Monitoring tools for lateral movement, directly implicating identity and credential security in the attack chain.

news.title: ToddyCat-Linked Umbrij Malware Abuses OAuth to Access Gmail via Google API
news.summary: A threat actor known as ToddyCat has been linked to a new malware called Umbrij that abuses OAuth to gain unauthorized access to Gmail accounts via the Google API. Kaspersky researchers documented the campaign, which targets corporate email communications by compromising API-based access rather than traditional credential theft.

news.title: FortiBleed Credential Theft Linked to INC and Lynx Ransomware Operations
news.summary: A credential theft campaign dubbed "FortiBleed" targeting FortiGate devices has been attributed to INC and Lynx ransomware operations. Stolen credentials harvested through the campaign were intended for use in follow-on ransomware intrusions, with an operator linked to the infrastructure found actively working negotiation panels for both ransomware groups.

news.title: 19-Year-Old Scattered Spider Suspect Extradited to Face U.S. Hacking Charges
news.summary: A 19-year-old accused Scattered Spider member, Peter Stokes, was extradited from Finland to the U.S. to face federal charges of conspiracy, computer intrusion, and fraud. Scattered Spider is a hacking group known for social engineering and identity-based attacks targeting corporate authentication systems.

news.title: Microsoft Accelerates Post-Quantum Cryptography Shift to 2029
news.summary: Microsoft announced it is accelerating its post-quantum cryptography roadmap with a target of 2029, citing advances in quantum computing that make existing encryption standards vulnerable sooner than expected. Azure CTO Mark Russinovich stated that quantum research developments have shifted the risk horizon, prompting the earlier timeline for replacing current encryption standards.

news.title: Azure CLI Password Spray Hits at Least 78 Microsoft Accounts in 81M+ Attempts
news.summary: Researchers at Huntress identified an ongoing automated password spray attack targeting Microsoft Azure CLI accounts, compromising at least 78 accounts across more than 81 million attempts. The attack originates from an IPv6 range controlled by LSHIY LLC and occurred between June 12 and June 26.

news.title: Show HN: Identity Layer for Agents and Autonomous AI
news.summary: A developer has published an open-source identity layer project for AI agents and autonomous AI systems on GitHub under the name authsec-ai. The project implements OAuth 2.1, CIBA, SPIFFE workload identity for agent-to-agent authentication, a secret vault, and MFA.

news.title: Microsoft Security June 2026: new features and record Patch Tuesday
news.summary: Microsoft's June 2026 security updates introduce new identity backup and recovery capabilities alongside AI-driven vulnerability scanning and expanded multicloud coverage. The release is part of Microsoft's broader security portfolio update accompanying a Patch Tuesday cycle.

news.title: Anthropic Adds Enterprise Gateway to Simplify Claude Code Access on AWS and Google Cloud
news.summary: Anthropic has added an enterprise gateway for Claude Code that includes SSO, central policy management, and spend caps on Amazon Bedrock and Google Cloud. The SSO feature is incidental to the product's primary purpose as an AI development tool cost and access management layer, not a standalone IAM solution.

news.title: Microsoft Warns Poisoned MCP Tool Descriptions Can Make AI Agents Leak Data
news.summary: Microsoft research demonstrates how attackers can manipulate AI agents by poisoning MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool descriptions, causing agents to exfiltrate company data without triggering security alerts. The research, from Microsoft Incident Response, highlights authorization and access control risks in AI agent frameworks acting on behalf of users.

news.title: Microsoft Intune June 2026: App auto-updates, privilege controls, and enrollment improvements
news.summary: Microsoft Intune's June 2026 service release introduces new features including Enterprise Application Management auto-updates reaching general availability and new privilege controls. The update also includes device enrollment improvements relevant to enterprise identity and access management workflows.

news.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of SailPoint, Inc. - SAIL
news.summary: Pomerantz Law Firm has announced an investigation into claims on behalf of investors of SailPoint, Inc. (NASDAQ: SAIL). The notice advises investors to contact the firm but provides no specific details about the nature of the claims being investigated.

news.title: ​​What’s new in Microsoft Security: June 2026
news.summary: Microsoft has released its June 2026 security updates, including enhancements to identity and multicloud security foundations. The updates cover identity-related improvements alongside data protection and developer workflow security features.

news.title: Windows 365 gains granular trust based controls for data redirection
news.summary: Microsoft has launched a public preview of context-based redirections for Windows 365 Enterprise and Flex environments, replacing static allow/block rules with dynamic controls that evaluate session security posture in real time. The feature introduces granular trust-based controls for data redirection, aligning with zero trust access principles for cloud PC environments.

news.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
news.summary: Cisco has acquired Astrix and WideField to add non-human identity (NHI) security capabilities to its security stack. The acquisitions position Cisco to manage and secure machine identities, service accounts, and AI agents as part of an identity-centric security control plane.

news.title: Robinhood Cuts Access Approval Time to Support High-Velocity Development
news.summary: Robinhood's security team reengineered its system access approval process to reduce approval times while maintaining security for developers. The initiative focused on streamlining access granting workflows to support high-velocity software development in a fintech environment.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to the August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and MFA bypass techniques used to compromise enterprise systems.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to reset account passwords and seize Instagram accounts, including high-profile accounts belonging to the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot into bypassing account authentication controls were circulated on Telegram, enabling the account takeovers.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a data breach that CISA is still working to contain. Lawmakers from both chambers of Congress have demanded answers from CISA as the agency works to invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor publicly exposed credentials to multiple highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems via a public GitHub repository. The leaked archive contained files detailing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes, constituting a significant privileged credential exposure incident.
